content_of_post_to_answer: How can I stop overthinking and relax my mind?
answer: Overthinking is often misunderstood as a problem of having too many thoughts. The deeper issue is usually that the mind is trying to create certainty where none exists. It keeps replaying conversations, predicting outcomes, and analyzing every detail because it believes one more thought will finally produce safety. A useful shift is not to fight thoughts, but to question the job you are giving them. Some thoughts solve problems; others only simulate control. Relaxation begins when you stop treating every thought as a task that needs to be completed.
